title: fragaria vesca f. roseiflora
intro: Fragaria vesca f. roseiflora, also known as the roseleaf wood strawberry or rose-leaf strawberry, is a subspecies of the wild strawberry (Fragaria vesca) native to Europe. It is a medicinal plant with anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, traditionally used to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Fragaria vesca f. roseiflora include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Fragaria vesca f. roseiflora can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, and its leaves and flowers are also used in herbal preparations, such as salves and creams, for topical application.
